channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Expelled from Paradise game announced
item.content:encoded:
Due out this winter in Japan.

Toei Animation has announced the development of a game based on the 2014 anime science-fiction film Expelled from Paradise. It will launch this winter in Japan. The official title, genre, supported platforms, and developer will be announced at a later date.

Set in the world of Expelled from Paradise, the game will feature a completely original story. Its release is being coordinated with the sequel film Expelled from Paradise: Resonance of the Heart, which is set to premiere on November 13.

Further information was not announced.

Here is an overview of the original Expelled from Paradise film, via Aniplex of America:

Gen Urobuchi (Nitroplus) and Seiji Mizushima collaborate to bring their first original theatrical feature utilizing state-of-the-art 3D CG.

Where did humanity come from? What defines humanity? Where will humanity go–? Expelled from Paradise demonstrates top-notch entertainment through the fusion of epic science fiction themes and breathtaking action scenes.

Directed by Seiji Mizushima (Fullmetal Alchemist, Mobile Suits Gundam 00) and written by Gen Urobuchi (Nitroplus) (Puella Magi Madoka Magica, Fate/Zero), Expelled from Paradise is the highly anticipated original theatrical feature project by these two critically-acclaimed creators.

The story takes place in the future when humanity’s new paradise, the cyber universe “DEVA’s” mainframe is hacked by a mysterious hacker from Earth. DEVA’s high officials dispatch Angela Balzac, a DEVA system security officer, down to the Earth’s surface to investigate the situations surrounding this mysterious hacker.

What are the hacker’s motives? Angela descends on to the devastated surface riding her exoskeletal powered suit Arhan.

Urobuchi’s amazing future world vision is brought to life by Mizushima and his talented creative staff through stunning visuals. The unique characters depicted in state-of-the-art 3DCG are voiced by a group of brilliant cast members.

Rie Kugimiya voices the heroine Angela Balzac, Shin-ichiro Miki as Dingo and Hiroshi Kamiya as Frontier Setter.

Prepare for an extraordinary journey to explore the secrets of the world…!



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/expelled-from-paradise-game-announced
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Wanderstop studio Ivy Road to shut down on March 31
item.content:encoded:
"One last surprise" still to come for Wanderstop.

Wanderstop developer Ivy Road will shut down on March 31, the studio announced.

“It’s hard to put into words how thankful we are to have been able to work together on Wanderstop—this is an incredible group of people!” the studio said. “And while we had a new project, Engine Angel, that we were excited about, unfortunately the funding didn’t come to fruition and the studio had to shut down.”

According to Ivy Road, there is still “one last surprise brewing” for Wanderstop, “one [the studio has] been working on for the last year to help find a way for Wanderstop to reach new players.” Publisher Annapurna Interactive will share more information about this in the future.

Wanderstop, which is available for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam, will remain available for purchase.

Here is the full message from Ivy Road on its closure:

Hi everyone,

Today, we must share some bittersweet news: Ivy Road is closing its doors as of March 31, 2026.

It’s hard to put into words how thankful we are to have been able to work together on Wanderstop—this is an incredible group of people! And while we had a new project, Engine Angel, that we were excited about, unfortunately the funding didn’t come to fruition and the studio had to shut down.

What Does This Mean for Wanderstop?

There’s still one last surprise brewing, one we’ve been working on for the last year to help find a way for Wanderstop to reach new players. The great folks at Annapurna Interactive will be sharing more news about this in the future, and we’re looking forward to you all seeing what it is!

The game will still be available to play and purchase on the platforms it’s currently available on. If you have future requests / feedback / bugs related to the game, please reach out to [email protected].

We also wanted to be sure to share a chapter select code that can be used to help if you’re a future player who happens to get stuck in an area—or if you just wanted to experience a specific chapter or moment of the game once more.

Putting in this code will open up a “Chapter Select” that allows you to start at the beginning of one of the game’s cycles.

How to Input the Chapter Select Code?

While in Chapter Select, using the D-pad, input: UP, LEFT, DOWN, RIGHT, UP, B (RMB), UP, RIGHT, DOWN, LEFT, UP, B (RMB), UP, DOWN, UP.

What Does This Mean for the Team and Engine Angel?

While we tried to shop the concept around and find a publishing partner, unfortunately we weren’t able to land a deal. It’s a particularly tough time for raising game funds, so while we weren’t necessarily surprised, we are disappointed that we won’t be able to bring Engine Angel to life together as a team. Liz’s concept is incredible, and we hope it may someday come to fruition!

For the team, this means many folks will be looking for work! If you’re in the position to recommend or hire developers for roles, please check out this thread on Bluesky for more info about the people on our team who’re currently actively looking for work!

THANK YOU to everyone who has supported Wanderstop and our team in any shape or form.

Making games is incredibly challenging work: it requires technical skills, emotional investment, financial investment, a whole lot of long hours, and a bit of luck and serendipitous timing thrown in.

We are so proud that we got to create something together as a team that was ultimately experienced by hundreds of thousands of players: we hope that you’re inspired to create something of your own someday, whether art, music, writing, or even a video game of your own.

Please remember to take care of yourselves—drink tea, make sure to rest, and take time to pet the pluffins.

—The Ivy Road Team



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/wanderstop-studio-ivy-road-to-shut-down-on-march-31
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
